claim_boundary.what_is_not_supported: holdout improvement, temporal transfer, cross-machine transfer, hardware execution, mitigation, soundness, quantum advantage, BQP separation, solved B4/B8/B10, or new credit
claim_boundary.what_is_supported: a deterministic foreign-route selector based only on public target calibration descriptors

requirements.label: R143, R125, R146 provenance, and task-builder hashes are bound
three targets and four tasks produce 12 adaptation groups
exactly two foreign candidates are evaluated per group
target-specific routes are excluded from every selector pool
selection uses target-descriptor exposure with deterministic tie breaks
all 24 foreign candidates preserve semantic fidelity
R146 hidden trial rows are never read for selection or tuning
no R147 holdout is executed during design
all candidate QASM files and hashes are replayable
hardware, advantage, solved-frontier, and credit claims remain false

selector.candidate_pool: the two foreign R143 routes for each target/task group
selector.primary_score: compiled combined_any_error_proxy on the target snapshot
selector.target_descriptor_source: R125 canonical target snapshot readout and CX calibration metadata

title: B4/B8 R147 target-descriptor foreign-route adaptation design
